results.description: Public input violation at isGitRepo:56: VIOLATION: The constructor accepts any string for projectRoo
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3 — Calling Context / Constructor Validation Gap
; VIOLATION: The constructor accepts any string for projectRoot with no validation
; that it is an absolute path. Node.js resolves relative cwd strings against
; process.cwd() inside execSync. The function then checks a different directory
; than the caller intended, and may return true for the wrong location.
;
; Code transition: this.projectRoot is stored as-is; execSync receives it as cwd
; without absolutization (no path.resolve() applied before the call).

(declare-const project_root_is_absolute  Int) ; 1 = absolute, 0 = relative
(declare-const resolved_dir_is_git_repo  Int) ; 1 = process.cwd()+relative is a git repo
(declare-const intended_dir_is_git_repo  Int) ; 1 = the caller's intended dir is a git repo
(declare-const exit_code                 Int) ; 0 = success, 1 = throws
(declare-const func_return               Int) ; 1 = true, 0 = false

; Code transition: execSync uses this.projectRoot directly as cwd
; When relative, the OS resolves it against process.cwd(), not the caller's intent
(assert (=> (= project_root_is_absolute 0) (= exit_code (- 1 resolved_dir_is_git_repo))))
; i.e., exit_code=0 iff resolved_dir_is_git_repo=1

; Code transition: function return is bound to exit_code
(assert (=> (= exit_code 0)       (= func_return 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= exit_code 0)) (= func_return 0)))

; Violation scenario: relative path, resolved dir IS a git repo, intended dir IS NOT
(assert (= project_root_is_absolute 0))
(assert (= resolved_dir_is_git_repo 1))
(assert (= intended_dir_is_git_repo 0))

; Consequence: func_return=1 but intended_dir_is_git_repo=0
(assert (not (= func_return intended_dir_is_git_repo)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: relative projectRoot="." when process.cwd() is inside a git repo,
; but the caller's intended directory is not. Function returns true incorrectly.

results.description: isGitRepo:56 → parseDiff:81: Proven: every file pushed to addedFiles is also in changedFi
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does isGitRepo:56 establish preconditions for parseDiff:81?
; Property: Proven: every file pushed to addedFiles is also in changedFiles.
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: every file pushed to addedFiles is also in changedFiles.
; Transition A: changedFiles.push(file) fires unconditionally once parts.length >= 2.
; Transition B: addedFiles.push(file) fires only when status starts with "A",
;               which requires the same parts.length >= 2 guard to have passed.
; Negation of property: file in addedFiles AND NOT in changedFiles → expect unsat.

(declare-const parts_length Int)
(declare-const status_starts_A Int)
(declare-const in_changed Int)
(declare-const in_added Int)

; Precondition: parts.length guard passes (line has at least 2 tab-separated parts)
(assert (>= parts_length 2))

; Transition A: changedFiles.push runs unconditionally when guard passes
(assert (=> (>= parts_length 2) (= in_changed 1)))

; Transition B: addedFiles.push runs only when status starts with "A"
(assert (=> (= status_starts_A 1) (= in_added 1)))
(assert (=> (= status_starts_A 0) (= in_added 0)))

; Scenario: status IS "A", file is in addedFiles
(assert (= status_starts_A 1))

; Negation: file NOT in changedFiles despite being in addedFiles
(assert (= in_changed 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — changedFiles.push always precedes addedFiles.push in the same guard scope

results.description: isGitRepo:56 → parseDiff:81: Proven: if execSync throws for any reason, parseDiff returns
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does isGitRepo:56 establish preconditions for parseDiff:81?
; Property: Proven: if execSync throws for any reason, parseDiff returns the initial
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: if execSync throws for any reason, parseDiff returns the initial
; empty result — all four arrays have zero elements.
; The bare `catch { return result }` catches all exception types.

(declare-const execsync_throws Int)
(declare-const changed_count Int)
(declare-const added_count Int)
(declare-const deleted_count Int)
(declare-const modified_count Int)

; Transition: catch block executes — returns result as initialized (all counts = 0)
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= changed_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= added_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= deleted_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= execsync_throws 1) (= modified_count 0)))

; Scenario: execSync throws
(assert (= execsync_throws 1))

; Negation: any array is non-empty despite exception
(assert (or (> changed_count 0)
            (> added_count 0)
            (> deleted_count 0)
            (> modified_count 0)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — the bare catch returns before any push can occur

results.description: isGitRepo:56 → parseDiff:81: Proven: if output is the empty string, the early return fire
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does isGitRepo:56 establish preconditions for parseDiff:81?
; Property: Proven: if output is the empty string, the early return fires before
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: if output is the empty string, the early return fires before
; the parsing loop, leaving all arrays at their initialized empty state.

(declare-const output_length Int)
(declare-const changed_count Int)
(declare-const added_count Int)

; Transition: `if (!output) return result` — empty string is falsy in JS/TS
(assert (=> (= output_length 0) (= changed_count 0)))
(assert (=> (= output_length 0) (= added_count 0)))

; Scenario: output is empty
(assert (= output_length 0))

; Negation: arrays non-empty despite empty output
(assert (or (> changed_count 0) (> added_count 0)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — early return precedes the split/loop entirely

results.description: Boundary violation at execute:75: REACHABLE: topologicalOrder.length = 0 at line 75 while file
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2, P6
; REACHABLE: topologicalOrder.length = 0 at line 75 while files.length > 0
; When input.changedFiles is non-empty but no changed path appears in the walked graph,
; findAffected's BFS starts from changedSet but finds no intersection with dependents map
; → affected = {} → filter reduces topologicalOrder to []
(declare-const files_len Int)
(declare-const changed_files_len Int)
(declare-const affected_size Int)
(declare-const topo_len Int)
; Code transition: walk() populated visited with at least one file
(assert (> files_len 0))
; Code transition: changedFiles guard passed — length > 0 branch taken
(assert (> changed_files_len 0))
; Code transition: findAffected BFS — changed paths are outside the project graph
; dependents map is keyed by walked import paths; changedSet paths are absent
(assert (= affected_size 0))
; Code transition: topologicalOrder.filter((p) => affected.has(p)) → all excluded
(assert (= topo_len affected_size))
; Violation: at line 75, derivation order is empty while file nodes exist
(assert (= topo_len 0))
(assert (> files_len 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — topologicalOrder = [] at line 75 with non-empty files
; The subsequent detail() call logs a misleading "Derivation order: " with no paths

results.description: Public input violation at log:18: REACHABLE: phaseNumber <= 0 — abstract property carries no p
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3, P5
; REACHABLE: phaseNumber <= 0 — abstract property carries no positivity contract.
; The log() method reads this.phaseNumber directly into the output label.
; A concrete subclass can assign any Int value.
;
; Transition: log() reads phase_number and embeds it as output_phase_label.
; That binding IS the code's only operation on phaseNumber here.

(declare-const phase_number       Int)
(declare-const output_phase_label Int)   ; the phase value written to the log line

; Transition: template literal embeds phaseNumber directly — no validation occurs
(assert (= output_phase_label phase_number))

; TypeScript 'number' imposes no lower bound; abstract subclass can assign freely
; No assert constrains phase_number from below

; Violation: the output label is semantically invalid (zero or negative phase)
(assert (<= output_phase_label 0))

(check-sat)
; EXPECT: sat — phase_number is unconstrained from below; output_phase_label <= 0 is reachable.
; Witness: phase_number = 0 → output "Phase 0: <message>"; phase_number = -1 → "Phase -1: ..."

results.description: Boundary violation at log:18: REACHABLE: message = "" produces a degenerate log entry "Pha
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; REACHABLE: message = "" produces a degenerate log entry "Phase N: " (no payload).
; TypeScript 'string' includes the empty string; log() performs no length check.
;
; Transition: output_length = 8 + digit_count + message_length
; When message_length = 0, the entire message contribution collapses to 0.

(declare-const phase_number   Int)
(declare-const digit_count    Int)
(declare-const message_length Int)
(declare-const output_length  Int)

; Transition: template literal string construction
(assert (= output_length (+ 8 digit_count message_length)))
(assert (>= phase_number 1))
(assert (>= digit_count  1))

; Degenerate input: empty string passes TypeScript type checking
(assert (= message_length 0))

; Violation: zero-length message payload — output carries no information
(assert (= (- output_length 8) digit_count))   ; content = only phase digits, no message

(check-sat)
; EXPECT: sat — message_length=0 is valid input; output degenerates to "Phase N: " with no body.
; A caller like log("") produces a structurally valid but informationally empty trace line.

results.description: log:18 → detail:22: PROVEN: output string length is always >= 2
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does log:18 establish preconditions for detail:22?
; Property: PROVEN: output string length is always >= 2
; PRINCIPLE: P6, P7
; PROVEN: output string length is always >= 2
; Template `  ${message}` prepends exactly 2 spaces.
; Transition: output_len = 2 + message_len, with message_len >= 0 (TypeScript string invariant)
; Negated property: output_len < 2
(declare-const message_len Int)
(declare-const output_len Int)
(assert (>= message_len 0))
(assert (= output_len (+ 2 message_len)))
(assert (< output_len 2))
(check-sat)
; expect unsat — output length cannot be less than 2

results.description: log:18 → detail:22: PROVEN: no arithmetic underflow or division in the template
results.smt2:
; P1 MECHANICAL: Does log:18 establish preconditions for detail:22?
; Property: PROVEN: no arithmetic underflow or division in the template literal construction
; PRINCIPLE: P1, P7
; PROVEN: no arithmetic underflow or division in the template literal construction
; The only operation is addition: output_len = 2 + message_len
; Negated property: output_len < 0
(declare-const message_len Int)
(declare-const output_len Int)
(assert (>= message_len 0))
(assert (= output_len (+ 2 message_len)))
(assert (< output_len 0))
(check-sat)
; expect unsat — addition of non-negative terms cannot go negative

results.description: Boundary violation at detail:22: REACHABLE: message = "" produces a degenerate whitespace-onl
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; REACHABLE: message = "" produces a degenerate whitespace-only log entry "  "
; Transition: output_len = 2 + message_len (fixed prefix "  " always present)
; Transition: meaningful_content_len = message_len (the 2-space prefix carries no payload)
; Violation: meaningful_content_len = 0, yet console.log fires and emits the line
(declare-const message_len Int)
(declare-const output_len Int)
(declare-const meaningful_content_len Int)
; Transition: template literal evaluation
(assert (= output_len (+ 2 message_len)))
; Transition: meaningful content is the message portion only
(assert (= meaningful_content_len message_len))
; Boundary input: caller passes empty string
(assert (= message_len 0))
; Consequence: log line has zero meaningful content
(assert (= meaningful_content_len 0))
; But output is still emitted (output_len = 2, not suppressed)
(assert (= output_len 2))
(check-sat)
; expect sat — witness: message_len=0, output_len=2, meaningful_content_len=0
; "  " is written to stdout: a whitespace-only line indistinguishable from an empty indent
